title: Umami Crispy Brazilian Picanha Feijoada
description: A rich and comforting Brazilian dish featuring the crispiest picanha ever, with a depth of umami from the feijoada sauce, and a hint of citrus from the lemon zest. Perfect for a cozy dinner with family and friends.
instructions: In a large Dutch oven, heat 2 tablespoons of butter over medium heat.
Add the sliced onion and cook until caramelized, stirring occasionally, about 20 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the dried black beans, beef broth, cumin, coriander, paprika, sugar, and salt. Bring to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 1 hour.
Rub the picanha beef with 1 tablespoon of butter, then season with black pepper and cayenne pepper.
In a separate pan, heat the remaining 2 tablespoons of butter over high heat.
Sear the picanha beef for 1-2 minutes per side, then transfer to the preheated oven and cook for 15-20 minutes, or until cooked to your desired level of doneness.
While the picanha is cooking, prepare the feijoada sauce by straining the bean mixture through a fine-mesh sieve into a blender or food processor.
Add the tomato paste, lemon zest, and flambe liquor to the blender and blend until smooth.
Return the bean mixture to the Dutch oven and stir in the feijoada sauce.
To serve, slice the picanha into thin strips and place on top of the feijoada. Garnish with chopped cilantro, sliced scallions, and a dollop of sour cream or yogurt.
